summary: Financial Email Notifications feature fully tested. All 25 backend tests pass. The financial_emails.py module is complete with all 5 email types, idempotency via MongoDB unique index, and non-blocking daemon threads. All hooks are properly integrated into attendance_service, distribution_service, and payout_service.

backend: MongoDB unique index 'unique_email_idempotency' exists on sent_emails collection with keys (email_type, reference_id, user_id)
Duplicate insert with same (email_type, reference_id, user_id) raises DuplicateKeyError
Idempotency logic prevents duplicate email records
All 5 email functions exist: send_capture_email, send_distribution_created_email, send_distribution_available_email, send_payout_completed_email, send_payout_failed_email
_already_sent and _mark_sent idempotency functions exist
_send_async uses daemon threads (threading.Thread with daemon=True)
_send_async checks _already_sent before sending (idempotent)
distribution_created email uses correct wording: 'crédit en attente' (not 'vous avez reçu')
Email subject contains 'Crédit en attente enregistré'
send_capture_email hook exists in attendance_service._execute_capture_and_distribution
send_distribution_created_email hook exists in distribution_service.create_distribution
distribution_created email only sent to 'organizer' and 'participant' roles (not platform/charity)
send_distribution_available_email hook exists in distribution_service._finalize_single_distribution
distribution_available email only sent when final_status == 'completed'
send_payout_completed_email hook exists in payout_service._execute_dev_payout
send_payout_completed_email hook exists in payout_service.handle_transfer_paid (webhook)
send_payout_failed_email hook exists in payout_service._execute_stripe_transfer (on StripeError)
send_payout_failed_email hook exists in payout_service.handle_transfer_failed (webhook)
server.py creates unique index on sent_emails in lifespan
GET /api/health returns 200 with status: healthy
send_capture_email has correct parameters (user_id, appointment_title, appointment_date, capture_amount_cents, distribution_id, beneficiaries, hold_expires_at)
send_distribution_created_email has correct parameters (user_id, role, amount_cents, appointment_title, appointment_date, distribution_id, hold_expires_at)
send_distribution_available_email has correct parameters (user_id, amount_cents, appointment_title, distribution_id)
send_payout_completed_email has correct parameters (user_id, amount_cents, payout_id, stripe_transfer_id)
send_payout_failed_email has correct parameters (user_id, amount_cents, payout_id, failure_reason)

idempotency.mechanism: MongoDB unique compound index on (email_type, reference_id, user_id)
idempotency.functions: _already_sent() checks if email exists, _mark_sent() records sent email
idempotency.behavior: Calling email function twice with same params does NOT send duplicate

non_blocking.behavior: Financial transactions never blocked by email sending

email_types.capture_no_show: Sent to no-show user when guarantee is captured
email_types.distribution_created: Sent to organizer/participant beneficiaries when distribution is created
email_types.distribution_available: Sent to beneficiaries when funds move from pending to available (after 15-day hold)
email_types.payout_completed: Sent when payout is successfully completed
email_types.payout_failed: Sent when payout fails (funds re-credited to wallet)

wording.distribution_created_email: Uses 'crédit en attente enregistré' (not 'vous avez reçu')
wording.subject: Crédit en attente enregistré — {appointment_title}

test_credentials: N/A - tests use source code inspection and direct MongoDB operations
seed_data_creation: None - tests use temporary test data that is cleaned up
context_for_next_testing_agent: Financial Email Notifications feature fully implemented and tested. Key components: (1) financial_emails.py module with 5 email functions + idempotency + threading. (2) MongoDB unique index 'unique_email_idempotency' on sent_emails collection with composite key (email_type, reference_id, user_id). (3) _send_async uses daemon threads to never block financial transactions. (4) Hooks integrated into attendance_service (capture), distribution_service (created/available), payout_service (completed/failed). (5) Email wording uses 'crédit en attente' not 'vous avez reçu'. (6) Resend API may fail silently but idempotency layer is tested.

mocked_apis_list: Resend email delivery (may fail silently - idempotency layer tested, not actual delivery)
